privileged_info_summary: The task uses a real, publicly available dataset from scikit-image's immunohistochemistry function (512×512×3 RGB image, 262,144 total pixels). The image contains genuine DAB (brown) and hematoxylin (blue) staining with approximately 45.22% DAB-positive area when subjected to proper color deconvolution (HED color space). The H DAB stain vector is a standard, documented stain vector in ImageJ/Fiji's Colour Deconvolution plugin. The metadata constraints (1-80% DAB fraction, minimum 1000 pixels = 0.381% of image) are biologically plausible for Ki-67 immunohistochemical analysis based on published literature showing Ki-67 proliferation indices typically ranging from <5% to >30% in clinical settings, with the upper bound of 80% providing margin for high-proliferation cases.
